definition: The third and posterior section of an insect body between the thorax and the tip; contains most digestive and reproductive organs.
A paired segmented sensory appendage on the head of an insect used to detect touch and odor.
The front pair of wings on a moth or butterfly; typically larger and more prominently patterned than the hindwing.
The rear pair of wings on a moth or butterfly; often concealed beneath the forewings at rest.
The middle section of an insect body between the head and abdomen; bears the legs and wings.
